question.label: Ask earlier
question.name: When you hold off asking, what tells you it is time to pull someone in?
question.description: Drills into his help-seeking threshold so the manager can make escalation expectations explicit.

answer: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.

assessment.note: He names specific, fast feedback as the condition that unlocks progress, which signals a strong clarity gap and some willingness to move once guidance is concrete.

new_queue.label: Specific and fast
new_queue.name: What kind of feedback helps most in those moments?

new_queue.label: Confidence trigger
new_queue.name: What tends to make you pause before shipping a ticket?

new_queue.label: Support cadence
new_queue.name: What kind of check-in from me would be most useful over the next two weeks?

new_queue.label: Two-week takeaway
new_queue.name: What’s the one thing you want to have changed by our next check-in?

issues: coverage: injected engagement into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
